formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
Here's a complete formula sheet for CBSE Class 11 English — Father to Son in JSON format:

```json
[
  {
    "name": "The Road Not Taken",
    "formula": "Two roads diverged in a yellow wood, And sorry I could not travel both, And be one traveler, long I stood And looked down one as far as I could, To where it bent in the undergrowth;",
    "variables": "Roads, traveler, yellow wood, undergrowth",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "A key poem in literature, emphasizing the importance of choice and its consequences."
  },
  {
    "name": "The Love Song of J. Alfred Prufrock",
    "formula": "Let us go then, you and I, When the evening is spread out against the sky, Like a patient etherized upon a table;",
    "variables": "Evening, sky, table, ether",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "A key poem in literature, exploring themes of anxiety and indecision."
  },
  {
    "name": "The Hollow Men",
    "formula": "We are the hollow men, We are the stuffed men, Leaning together, Headpiece filled with straw.",
    "variables": "Hollow men, stuffed men, straw, headpiece",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "A key poem in literature, exploring themes of disillusionment and emptiness."
  },
  {
    "name": "The Waste Land",
    "formula": "April is the cruellest month, breeding Lilacs out of the dead land, mixing Memory and desire, stirring Dull roots with spring rain.",
    "variables": "April, month, Lilacs, land, Memory, desire, roots, spring rain",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "A key poem in literature, exploring themes of disillusionment and spiritual decay."
  },
  {
    "name": "The Tyger (from Songs of Experience)",
    "formula": "Did he who made the Lamb make thee? Blind as he was, yet he put forth the piercing eye That on the peaks and pinnacles of old Towers at their base the deep and crumbled deep, In the green valley of the wood, where he was born.",
    "variables": "Tyger, Lamb, eye, peaks, pinnacles, old, wood, valley",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "A key poem in literature, exploring themes of creation and morality."
  },
  {
    "name": "Ozymandias",
    "formula": "Round the decay Of that colossal Wreck, boundless and bare The lone and level sands Stretch far away.",
    "variables": "Decay, wreck, sands, level",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "A key poem in literature, exploring themes of power and decline."
  },
  {
    "name": "The Raven",
    "formula": "Once upon a midnight dreary, while I pondered, weak and weary, Over many a quaint and curious volume of forgotten lore— While I nodded, nearly napping, suddenly there came a tapping, As of someone gently rapping, rapping at my chamber door.",
    "variables": "Midnight, volume, tapping, door",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "A key poem in literature, exploring themes of love and loss."
  },
  {
    "name": "The Lady of Shalott",
    "formula": "On either side the river lie Long fields of barley and of rye, That clothe the wold and meet the sky; And thro' the fields the road runs by To many-tower'd Camelot; And up and down the people go, Gazing where the lilies blow Round an island there below, The island of Shalott.",
    "variables": "River, fields, wold, road, Camelot, people, island",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "A key poem in literature, exploring themes of isolation and longing."
  },
  {
    "name": "To Autumn",
    "formula": "Season of mists and mellow fruitfulness, Conspiring with him how to load and bless With fruit the vines that round the thatch-eves run;",
    "variables": "Season, mists, fruitfulness, vines, thatch-eves",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "A key poem in literature, exploring themes of harvest and nature."
  },
  {
    "name": "Ode to a Nightingale",
    "formula": "My heart aches, and a drowsy numbness pains My sense, as though of hemlock I had drunk, Or emptied some dull opiate to the drains One minute past, and Lethe-wards had sunk: 'Tis not through envy of thy happy lot, But being too happy in thy happiness,— That thou, light-winged Dryad of the trees, In some melodious plot Of beechen green, and shadows numberless, Singest of summer in full-throated ease.",
    "variables": "Heart, hemlock, opiate, drains, Lethe, trees, Dryad, beechen",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "A key poem in literature, exploring themes of mortality and the fleeting nature of life."
  },
  {
    "name": "Do Not Go Gentle into That Good Night",
    "formula": "Do not go gentle into that good night, Old age should burn and rave at close of day; Rage, rage against the dying of the light.",
    "variables": "Night, age, light",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "A key poem in literature, exploring themes of mortality
